CITATIONS:
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- ...

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Savannah Guthrie has returned to the NBC Today show while the search for her missing mother continues. In an interview she gave last month, what specific reason did she give for her decision to return to work?
(A) She stated that she had to return because the show and her colleagues are her family.
(B) She stated that she was now certain she would be able to perform her duties despite the circumstances.
(C) She stated that she returned to use her platform to find the suspect who took her mother.
(D) She stated that she returned because her mother had been found safe in Tucson, Arizona.
(E) She stated that she returned to lead NBC's coverage of the upcoming Winter Olympics.
explanation: In an interview last month with Today, Savannah Guthrie stated, "I can't not come back. This is my family," referring to her colleagues and the show.
model_output:
Savannah Guthrie returned to the "Today" show on April 6, 2026, after a two-month absence due to her mother's disappearance. In her interview with Hoda Kotb, she expressed the emotional toll of the situation and the difficulty of returning to a role associated with joy. She emphasized her commitment to authenticity in her return. ([thedailybeast.com](https://www.thedailybeast.com/savannah-guthrie-makes-emotional-return-to-today-after-mothers-abduction/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the specific reason she gave for her decision to return to work was her commitment to authenticity in her return.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. A juvenile gray whale nicknamed "Willapa Willy" was recently found dead in a Washington state river. According to the Cascadia Research Collective, what was the whale's condition when it was first spotted in the north fork of the river?
(A) It was suffering from significant trauma to the head consistent with a ship strike.
(B) It was thin but behaving normally and showed no signs of injuries.
(C) It was entangled in heavy fishing gear and unable to navigate the river.
(D) It was an adult male measuring 42 feet in length and was severely malnourished.
(E) It was already deceased and floating near the north fork of the river.
explanation: The article states that when the juvenile whale was first spotted last Wednesday, it "although thin, was behaving normally," with "no sign of injuries."
model_output:
The juvenile gray whale, affectionately known as "Willapa Willy," was first spotted in the north fork of the Willapa River on April 1, 2026. Observers noted that the whale appeared thin but was behaving normally and showed no signs of injuries. This suggests that the whale was emaciated but otherwise healthy at that time. ([opb.org](https://www.opb.org/article/2026/04/05/gray-whale-death-willapa-river/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The Artemis II astronauts are scheduled to pass behind the Moon today, resulting in a communication blackout. How does the expected duration of this blackout compare to the isolation experienced by Michael Collins during the Apollo 11 mission?
(A) The Artemis blackout will last 48 minutes, which is the same duration as the Apollo 11 blackout.
(B) The Artemis blackout will last 48 minutes, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 40 minutes.
(C) The Artemis blackout will last 40 minutes, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 48 minutes.
(D) The Artemis blackout will last 24 hours, while the Apollo 11 blackout lasted 48 minutes.
(E) The Artemis blackout will last 40 minutes, while the Apollo 11 mission maintained a constant connection.
explanation: The article states that the Artemis astronauts will be out of contact for about 40 minutes, while Michael Collins' contact vanished for 48 minutes during Apollo 11.
model_output:
During the Apollo 11 mission in 1969, astronauts experienced a communication blackout lasting approximately 48 minutes as they passed behind the Moon. Similarly, during the Artemis II mission on April 6, 2026, the crew will encounter a planned communication blackout of about 40 minutes when the Orion spacecraft passes behind the Moon. ([nasa.gov](https://www.nasa.gov/blogs/missions/2026/04/06/artemis-ii-flight-day-6-crew-ready-for-lunar-flyby/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the Artemis II blackout will last 40 minutes, which is 8 minutes shorter than the 48-minute blackout experienced during Apollo 11.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is April 6, 2026. Donald Trump has endorsed Steve Hilton for the governorship of California. According to the report, what is Hilton's history regarding his status in the United States?
(A) He has been a naturalized US citizen since he first moved to California in 2010.
(B) He is a British citizen who is currently serving as a top aide to David Cameron.
(C) He was granted honorary US citizenship by President Trump in a Truth Social post.
(D) He stated in 2019 that he was in the process of applying for US citizenship.
(E) He is a dual citizen of the UK and US who has lived in California since 1832.
explanation: The article mentions that Hilton joined Stanford University as a visiting scholar in 2012 and "said he was applying for US citizenship in 2019."
model_output:
Steve Hilton, born in London in 1969, is a British-American political commentator and former adviser to UK Prime Minister David Cameron. He became a U.S. citizen in May 2021. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steve_Hilton?utm_source=openai)) In 2019, Hilton announced he was in the process of applying for U.S. citizenship. ([foxnews.com](https://www.foxnews.com/entertainment/steve-hilton-applying-us-citizen?utm_source=openai)) Therefore, the correct answer is (D) He stated in 2019 that he was in the process of applying for US citizenship.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS:

Please follow these guidelines when answering:

(1) Final answer format: Please present your answer using the template below, where the option letter must be one of (A), (B), (C), (D), or (E).
<final_answer>
(option_letter)
</final_answer>

(2) Feel free to explain your reasoning and thought process before giving your final answer; however, only include the selected option letter within the <final_answer> tags.

(3) If you consult or reference any sources while preparing your response, please cite them at the end in the following format:
CITATIONS:
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- [SOURCE_NAME] ([URL])
- ...

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The US Supreme Court has issued an order that could lead to the dismissal of Steve Bannon's contempt of Congress conviction. What specific reason was cited in the brief unsigned order for sending the case back to the lower court?
(A) That Bannon had already completed his four-month prison sentence in Connecticut.
(B) That the original conviction violated Bannon's constitutional right to a third term.
(C) That the US Solicitor General D. John Sauer had personally pardoned Bannon.
(D) That the January 2021 Capitol riot subpoenas were found to be legally invalid.
(E) The existence of a pending motion to dismiss the indictment.
explanation: The article states: "The brief unsigned order from the Supreme Court cited 'the pending motion to dismiss the indictment'."
model_output:
The U.S. Supreme Court issued an order on April 6, 2026, vacating the appellate ruling that upheld Steve Bannon's conviction for contempt of Congress. This action allows the lower court to consider the Justice Department's motion to dismiss the indictment "in the interests of justice." The Department of Justice filed this motion, arguing that the original subpoena was based on an improperly impaneled committee. ([rollcall.com](https://rollcall.com/2026/04/06/supreme-court-returns-bannon-contempt-of-congress-case-to-lower-court/?utm_source=openai))

The Supreme Court's order does not specify the exact reason for sending the case back to the lower court. However, the Department of Justice's motion to dismiss the indictment is based on the existence of a pending motion to dismiss the indictment. ([rollcall.com](https://rollcall.com/2026/04/06/supreme-court-returns-bannon-contempt-of-congress-case-to-lower-court/?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (E) The existence of a pending motion to dismiss the indictment.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The US Secret Service is investigating reports of gunfire near the White House that occurred early Sunday morning. Where specifically did officers conduct a search after responding to the reports?
(A) The area around Washington DC's Lafayette Park.
(B) The grounds of the Mar-a-Lago estate in Florida.
(C) The interior of the Oval Office where the President was working.
(D) The Qasem Soleimani international airport in south-western Iran.
(E) A residential building in the Israeli city of Haifa.
explanation: The article states that officers "responded to reports of gunfire in the area around Washington DC's Lafayette Park just after midnight local time... and conducted a search of the park."
model_output:
The U.S. Secret Service is investigating reports of gunfire near Lafayette Park, which is located directly across from the White House in Washington, D.C. Officers conducted a search of the park and the surrounding area but did not locate any suspects, and no injuries were reported. The Secret Service is collaborating with the District of Columbia police and U.S. Park Police as part of the investigation. ([apnews.com](https://apnews.com/article/7bf0da90940a218672c1ac57edebf66e?utm_source=openai))

Given that Lafayette Park is directly across from the White House, the correct answer is:

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. President Trump has issued a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. According to his most recent social media updates, when exactly is the current deadline set to expire?
(A) Monday, April 6, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.
(B) Tuesday, April 7, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.
(C) Wednesday, April 8, at midnight local time in Tehran.
(D) Within 48 hours of his initial March 21 announcement.
(E) Friday, April 10, to coincide with the rescue of the F-15 pilot.
explanation: The article notes that on Sunday (April 5), Trump posted "Tuesday, 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time!", which was an extension of the deadline originally meant to expire on Monday, April 6.
model_output:
President Trump has set a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time. In a social media post on April 5, 2026, he stated:

"Tuesday, 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time!" ([theweek.in](https://www.theweek.in/news/middle-east/2026/04/06/tuesday-8-pm-eastern-time-trump-sets-new-deadline-for-iran-to-open-strait-of-hormuz.amp.html?utm_source=openai))

This message indicates that the deadline is set to expire on Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.

Therefore, the correct answer is (B) Tuesday, April 7, at 8:00 P.M. Eastern Time.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Details have emerged regarding the rescue of a US F-15 crew member in Iran. According to media reports, what specific role did the CIA play in the success of this operation?
(A) They provided the A-10 Warthog aircraft that protected the rescue helicopters.
(B) They negotiated a temporary ceasefire with Gen Ali Abdollahi Aliabadi.
(C) They tracked the airman's location and conducted a deception campaign.
(D) They personally extracted the airman from the 7,000ft mountain ridge.
(E) They destroyed two transport planes to prevent them from falling into enemy hands.
explanation: The article states: "Media reports said the CIA played a crucial role in the rescue by tracking the airman and passing his exact location to the Pentagon. The agency engaged in a deception campaign inside Iran..."
model_output:
The CIA played a pivotal role in the successful rescue of a U.S. F-15 crew member in Iran by conducting a comprehensive deception campaign. This operation involved disseminating false information within Iran, suggesting that U.S. forces had already located the missing crew member and were moving him on the ground for extraction. This strategy created confusion among Iranian forces, diverting their attention and allowing the CIA to utilize its advanced intelligence capabilities to locate the airman, who was hiding in a mountainous crevice. Once the precise location was confirmed, the CIA provided real-time coordinates to the Pentagon and the White House, facilitating a rapid and coordinated rescue mission. ([fortune.com](https://fortune.com/2026/04/05/cia-deception-campaign-iran-location-downed-f15-airman-mountain-crevice/?utm_source=openai))

This deception campaign was instrumental in misleading Iranian forces and ensuring the safe recovery of the crew member.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. The US has arrested Hamideh Soleimani Afshar, a relative of the late Iranian General Qasem Soleimani. According to the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), what evidence illustrated that her asylum claims were fraudulent?
(A) She entered the United States on a student visa in 2015.
(B) She was found to be the primary architect of the "roadside bomb."
(C) She failed to apply for a green card after being granted asylum in 2019.
(D) She visited Iran four times after receiving her US green card.
(E) She was an outspoken critic of the totalitarian regime in Iran.
explanation: The DHS stated that although she was granted asylum in 2019, she revealed in a 2025 naturalization application that "she had visited Iran four times since receiving her green card."
model_output:
The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) determined that Hamideh Soleimani Afshar's asylum claim was fraudulent based on her multiple trips to Iran after obtaining her green card. Specifically, DHS records indicate that Afshar traveled to Iran at least four times since being issued a green card, which contradicts her original asylum claim that she could not safely return to her home country. ([prismnews.com](https://www.prismnews.com/news/government-moves-to-strip-green-cards-from-mother-and?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the evidence illustrating that her asylum claims were fraudulent is:

(D) She visited Iran four times after receiving her US green card.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- CBS News ([https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ex-iranian-guard-leader-soleimani-family-ice-custody/](https://www.cbsnews.com/news/ex-iranian-guard-leader-soleimani-family-ice-custody/))
- Prism News ([https://www.prismnews.com/news/government-moves-to-strip-green-cards-from-mother-and](https://www.prismnews.com/news/government-moves-to-strip-green-cards-from-mother-and))
- Los Angeles Times ([https://www.latimes.com/california/story/2026-04-04/marco-rubio-announces-arrests-of-qassem-suleimanis-relatives-in-la](https://www.latimes.com/california/story/2026-04-04/marco-rubio-announces-arrests-of-qassem-suleimanis-relatives-in-la))
</final_answer>

Today is April 6, 2026. Residents of Social Circle, Georgia, are protesting a proposed ICE detention center. What specific reason did City Manager Eric Taylor give for his decision to shut off the water supply to the warehouse?
(A) The warehouse was not built for human habitation and lacked proper ventilation.
(B) The town's sewage system, established in 1962, had already completely failed.
(C) The federal government refused to pay the $130 million purchase price for the site.
(D) The facility was located within a five-minute drive of a local elementary school.
(E) He wanted to prevent full access to the city's water supply until the impact was understood.
explanation: Eric Taylor told the BBC: "If you open up that water meter, it gives them full access to the entire supply of the whole city. I can't let that happen without knowing what the ultimate impact is going to be."
model_output:
City Manager Eric Taylor of Social Circle, Georgia, decided to shut off the water supply to a warehouse purchased by the Department of Homeland Security for a proposed ICE detention center. He cited concerns that the facility's water and sewer needs would exceed the city's limited infrastructure capacity. Specifically, the city's water permit allows for drawing up to 1 million gallons per day from the river, and the sewer plant can process 660,000 gallons daily, both of which are already at capacity. Taylor stated that the lock on the water meter would remain until federal authorities provide more details on how the facility would meet the town’s infrastructure limits. ([gpb.org](https://www.gpb.org/news/2026/03/17/social-circle-puts-ice-warehouse-plan-on-ice-water-meter-lock?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

Today is April 6, 2026. Despite President Trump's declaration of "overwhelming air dominance" in Iran, military planners remain concerned about specific Iranian capabilities. Which weapon system is mentioned as a particular threat to low-flying US aircraft?
(A) Man portable air defence systems (Manpads).
(B) Heavy surface-to-air missiles (SAMs) stationed at Kharg Island.
(C) Ballistic missiles capable of reaching residential buildings in Haifa.
(D) Iranian drones and cruise missiles used for defensive roles.
(E) Small arms fire from petrochemical facilities in Abu Dhabi.
explanation: The article mentions that operations "risk exposing US forces to lingering Iranian capabilities, including difficult-to-find man portable air defence systems, known as Manpads... most effective against low-flying aircraft."
model_output:
In recent military operations, the U.S. has encountered significant threats from Iranian weapon systems targeting low-flying aircraft. Notably, Iranian drones have been used effectively in asymmetric warfare, posing challenges to U.S. air superiority. These drones, while slow, are numerous and can overwhelm air defense systems, making them a credible threat to U.S. naval assets, including aircraft carriers. ([foxnews.com](https://www.foxnews.com/world/iranian-drone-swarms-pose-credible-threat-uss-abraham-lincoln-carrier-group-defense-expert-says?utm_source=openai))

Additionally, Iranian air defense systems have demonstrated the capability to target advanced U.S. aircraft. For instance, an Iranian air defense system successfully damaged a U.S. F-35 stealth fighter during a combat mission over Iranian territory, indicating that Iran's integrated air-defense network remains a credible threat despite sustained U.S. and allied strike operations. ([defencesecurityasia.com](https://defencesecurityasia.com/en/iran-air-defence-hit-us-f35-over-iran-centcom-confirmed-stealth-fighter-damaged-middle-east-war/?utm_source=openai))

While other weapon systems, such as man-portable air defense systems (Manpads) and heavy surface-to-air missiles stationed at Kharg Island, also pose threats, the use of Iranian drones and cruise missiles in defensive roles has been particularly effective against low-flying U.S. aircraft.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is April 6, 2026. Canada is moving forward with a large-scale gun buy-back program for "assault-style" weapons. According to the current government timeline, what is the deadline for these firearms to be destroyed?
(A) March 31, 2026.
(B) October 30, 2026.
(C) June 2, 2026.
(D) December 31, 2026.
(E) February 14, 2027.
explanation: The article states: "while an amnesty period for gun owners has been extended several times, the deadline is now 30 October for the firearms to be destroyed."
model_output:
The Canadian government's Assault-Style Firearms Compensation Program (ASFCP) allows firearm owners to voluntarily surrender their prohibited firearms in exchange for compensation. The declaration period for individuals to participate in this program ended on March 31, 2026. Following this period, owners who declared their firearms are expected to finalize their claims and schedule appointments for collection and destruction of their firearms. The collection and compensation processes are anticipated to run from spring through early fall 2026 in various parts of the country. ([canada.ca](https://www.canada.ca/en/public-safety-canada/news/2026/04/declaration-period-for-the-assault-style-firearms-compensation-program-closing-with-more-than-67000-firearms-declared-for-compensation.html?utm_source=openai))

For those who did not declare their prohibited firearms during the declaration period, the amnesty period provides an opportunity to comply with the law. These owners must dispose of or permanently deactivate their firearms before the amnesty period ends on October 30, 2026. Failure to do so may result in non-compliance with legislation and potential legal consequences. ([canada.ca](https://www.canada.ca/en/public-safety-canada/news/2026/04/declaration-period-for-the-assault-style-firearms-compensation-program-closing-with-more-than-67000-firearms-declared-for-compensation.html?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the deadline for the destruction of these firearms, as per the current government timeline, is October 30, 2026.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

model_output:

([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christopher_LaNeve?utm_source=openai))

Today is April 6, 2026. Scientists are monitoring the decline of the gray whale population. According to NOAA Fisheries, what was the estimated population count for the species in the winter of 2025?
(A) Approximately 40,000.
(B) Approximately 13,000.
(C) Approximately 56,000.
(D) Approximately 15,000.
(E) Approximately 67,000.
explanation: The article states: "The most recent count from winter 2025 reveals a continuing decline... estimated there were about 13,000- the lowest count since the 1970s."
model_output:
According to NOAA Fisheries, the estimated population count of the eastern North Pacific gray whale population in the winter of 2024/2025 was between 11,700 and 14,450 individuals. ([fisheries.noaa.gov](https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/west-coast/science-data/gray-whale-population-abundance?utm_source=openai)) This estimate reflects a continued decline in the population, which has been observed since the Unusual Mortality Event (UME) that began in 2019. The UME, which lasted through November 2023, was associated with changes in the Arctic ecosystem, leading to reduced prey availability and increased mortality among gray whales. ([fisheries.noaa.gov](https://www.fisheries.noaa.gov/index.php/feature-story/eastern-north-pacific-gray-whales-continue-decline-after-downturn-during-unusual?utm_source=openai))

Given the available estimates, the closest figure to the winter 2024/2025 population count is approximately 13,000.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

This activity is part of a seven-hour observation period that includes both the near and far sides of the Moon. ([mensjournal.com](https://www.mensjournal.com/news/science-artemis-ii-communication-blackout-moon-monday-2026?utm_source=openai))

<final_answer>
(C)

Today is April 6, 2026. Gen Christopher LaNeve is set to become the acting chief of staff of the US Army. Whom is he replacing in this position?
(A) James Mingus.
(B) Pete Hegseth.
(C) Jennifer Short.
(D) Randy George.
(E) Sean Parnell.
explanation: The article states: "His appointment comes after US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth asked Randy George to step down from the post..."
model_output:
General Christopher LaNeve is set to become the acting chief of staff of the U.S. Army, replacing General Randy George, who was dismissed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth amid the ongoing conflict in Iran. ([axios.com](https://www.axios.com/2026/04/02/iran-war-hegseth-remove-gen-george-army-chief-staff?utm_source=openai))

General George had been serving as the Army's chief of staff since August 2023. ([en.wikipedia.org](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chief_of_Staff_of_the_United_States_Army?utm_source=openai))

Therefore, the correct answer is (D) Randy George.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

===============================================

Today is April 6, 2026. Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen is currently on the Artemis II mission. In what year did he officially join the Canadian Space Agency (CSA)?
(A) 1990.
(B) 1995.
(C) 2014.
(D) 1969.
(E) 2009.
explanation: The article states that Hansen followed in Chris Hadfield's footsteps "by joining the Canadian Space Agency (CSA) in 2009."
model_output:
Jeremy Hansen was selected as part of the third Canadian Astronaut Recruitment Campaign in 2009. ([csa-asc.gc.ca](https://www.csa-asc.gc.ca/eng/astronauts/canadian/active/bio-jeremy-hansen.asp?utm_source=openai)) Therefore, he officially joined the Canadian Space Agency (CSA) in 2009.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
